title: Ornithogalum minor
taxonomy: Ornithogalum minor is a species of flowering plant within the family Asparagaceae. It belongs to the genus Ornithogalum, which consists of approximately 200 species of bulbs. Ornithogalum minor is native to Europe and Asia, where it grows in a variety of habitats, including grasslands and rocky outcrops. The plant produces white, bell-shaped flowers in the summer months, which are a source of nectar for pollinators. The species is also known as the lesser star of Bethlehem, due to its star-shaped flowers.

intro: Ornithogalum minor, also known as the white star of Bethlehem, is a perennial plant in the Asparagaceae family that is native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis, wounds, and menstrual cramps.
